claims.claim_text: Autoregressive text generation iteratively predicts the next token from a probability distribution conditioned on prior context until a stopping condition is reached.
claims.rationale: The Hugging Face LLM tutorial explicitly states that autoregressive generation iteratively selects the next token from the model's next-token probability distribution until a stop condition is reached.

claims.claim_text: Top-p, or nucleus sampling, keeps the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches the threshold.
claims.span_hint: section: Top-p: Dynamic Filtering With a Nucleus
claims.rationale: The Transformers generation docs define `top_p` in cumulative-probability terms that match this claim.

claims.claim_text: Top-k sampling keeps exactly the k highest-probability tokens and removes the rest.
claims.span_hint: section: Top-k: Static Filtering and Its Trade-Offs
claims.rationale: The same generation reference defines `top_k` as keeping the highest-probability vocabulary tokens for top-k filtering.

claims.claim_text: Even with temperature set to zero, provider-side behavior can still affect repeatability.
claims.anchor_text: Some APIs also have model-side nondeterminism, backend changes, safety layers, or tool-use routing that can affect repeatability even when temperature is zero.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Some APIs can still show provider-side variability even when temperature is zero.
claims.rationale: Provider-side nondeterminism is real, but the sentence's full list of mechanisms is broader than what a single allowlisted primary source directly supports.

claims.claim_text: The 2019 paper "The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration" argued that likelihood-maximizing decoding can produce bland or repetitive text and motivated nucleus sampling.
claims.rationale: The paper abstract directly says likelihood as a decoding objective leads to bland and repetitive text and that the findings motivate nucleus sampling.

claims.claim_text: A zero-temperature setting is not a universal reproducibility guarantee.
claims.anchor_text: A zero-temperature setting is best understood as a decoding choice, not as a universal reproducibility guarantee, because provider infrastructure, model versions, safety layers, and tool routing can still change behavior.
claims.suggested_rewrite: A zero-temperature setting is best understood as a decoding choice, not as a universal reproducibility guarantee, because provider-side behavior can still affect repeatability.
claims.rationale: The general point is supportable, but the exact mechanism list is too specific for a clean allowlisted citation.

claims.claim_text: Repetition problems often appear only after several hundred generated tokens.
claims.anchor_text: Repetition problems often become visible only after several hundred generated tokens, which is why short demos can look healthy while long-form production responses still degrade.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Repetition problems often become visible only in longer outputs, which is why short demos can look healthy while long-form production responses still degrade.
claims.rationale: The long-output warning is reasonable, but the specific token-count threshold is not directly backed by an allowlisted primary source.

claims.claim_text: Combining top-p and top-k can behave differently across backends depending on implementation details such as filter order.
claims.span_hint: section: Top-k: Static Filtering and Its Trade-Offs
claims.anchor_text: Some systems apply top-k first, then top-p. Others apply nucleus filtering first, then cap the result.
claims.suggested_rewrite: If you combine top-p and top-k, consult the provider documentation and test the combination directly before relying on it in production.
claims.rationale: The warning is useful, but the sentence asserts specific cross-backend ordering differences without an allowlisted primary source demonstrating both behaviors.

further_reading.title: The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary paper for nucleus sampling and degeneration under likelihood-maximizing decoding.

further_reading.title: Hugging Face Transformers: Text Generation Strategies
further_reading.why_relevant: Practical reference for greedy decoding, sampling, and current generation terminology.

notes: Quiz items, code blocks, tables, and lab mechanics were excluded unless they embedded a standalone factual claim. Most numeric parameter presets were treated as pedagogical starting points rather than cite-every-number claims, except where the text made a historical, provider-behavior, or paper-specific assertion.
